Paragraph

Suppose that \(A\) is an \(m\times n\) matrix with rank \(n\text{,}\) and that \(m\ge n\text{.}\)
  1. Prove that there exists some \(\lambda \gt 0\) such that \(\Vert A\bx \Vert \ge \lambda \Vert\bx \Vert\) for all \(\bx\in \bbR^{n}\text{.}\)
  2. Prove that there exists some \(\delta >0\) such that any \(m\times n\) matrix \(B\) satisfying \(\Vert B-A\Vert \lt \delta\) also has rank \(n\text{.}\)
in-context
